<svg viewBox="0 0 200 200" width="100%" height="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
      <defs>
        <linearGradient id="grad-hu-1" x1="0%" y1="0%" x2="100%" y2="100%">
          <stop offset="0%" stop-color="hsl(17, 70%, 50%)" />
          <stop offset="100%" stop-color="hsl(137, 70%, 50%)" />
        </linearGradient>
      </defs>
      <rect width="200" height="200" fill="#0b0b1e" />
      <!-- Clean, symmetrical, balanced vector illustration -->
      <g transform="translate(100,100)">
        <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="65" fill="none" stroke="url(#grad-hu-1)" stroke-width="3" />
        <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="50" fill="none" stroke="#00f2fe" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4,4" />
        <!-- Symmetric cross and inner square -->
        <line x1="-80" y1="0" x2="80" y2="0" stroke="url(#grad-hu-1)" stroke-width="1.5" opacity="0.6" />
        <line x1="0" y1="-80" x2="0" y2="80" stroke="url(#grad-hu-1)" stroke-width="1.5" opacity="0.6" />
        <rect x="-35" y="-35" width="70" height="70" fill="none" stroke="#fff" stroke-width="2" transform="rotate(45)" opacity="0.9" />
        <!-- Symmetrical nodes -->
        <circle cx="0" cy="-50" r="4" fill="#fff" />
        <circle cx="0" cy="50" r="4" fill="#fff" />
        <circle cx="-50" cy="0" r="4" fill="#fff" />
        <circle cx="50" cy="0" r="4" fill="#fff" />
        <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="10" fill="url(#grad-hu-1)" />
      </g>
    </svg>
hint: Look at the perfect symmetry and structural consistency of the lines.
explanation: The design is highly structured, exhibits perfect symmetry, and shows a deliberate grid alignment created by a human artist.
